message.content:
{
  "analysis": "Pip is now installed in the system Python, but torch/transformers are still missing. The next required action is to install CPU PyTorch and transformers so I can implement and behaviorally verify the pipeline parallel function. Since this is an externally managed environment, I will use --break-system-packages. I will install torch from the PyTorch CPU index first, then transformers from PyPI, then verify imports, distributed gloo availability, and tiny Llama model layer access.",
  "plan": "1. Install CPU torch using the official CPU wheel index with --break-system-packages.
2. Install transformers from PyPI with --break-system-packages.
3. Verify torch/transformers imports, gloo availability, and LlamaForCausalLM layer structure. These are prerequisites for writing and running a real multi-process self-test.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 -m pip install --break-system-packages --no-cache-dir torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu 2>&1 | tail -40
",
      "duration": 60.0
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 -m pip install --break-system-packages --no-cache-dir transformers 2>&1 | tail -40
",
      "duration": 60.0
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 - <<'PY'
import torch, transformers
from transformers import LlamaForCausalLM, LlamaConfig
print('torch', torch.__version__)
print('transformers', transformers.__version__)
print('cuda', torch.cuda.is_available())
print('gloo ok', torch.distributed.is_gloo_available())
cfg = LlamaConfig(vocab_size=128, hidden_size=32, intermediate_size=64, num_hidden_layers=4, num_attention_heads=4, max_position_embeddings=16)
m = LlamaForCausalLM(cfg)
print('layers', len(m.model.layers))
print('embed tokens', m.model.embed_tokens)
print('lm head', m.lm_head)
print('params', sum(p.numel() for p in m.parameters()))
PY
",
      "duration": 1.0
    }
  ]
}
